title: Adobe’s Project Indigo camera app gains experimental AI photo editing tools
excerpt: Adobe is rolling out a limited test today that gives select Project Indigo users access to several generative AI-powered photo-editing tools.
content_html:
Adobe is rolling out a limited test today that gives select Project Indigo users access to several generative AI-powered photo-editing tools. Here are the details.

Adobe’s Project Indigo gets an experimental AI Playground

A little more than a year ago, Adobe announced Project Indigo, a free experimental iPhone camera app offering full manual controls and a more natural, SLR-like approach to computational photography.

Last April, the company expanded Project Indigo to select iPads and the iPhone 17e, in an update that also introduced a new grid view, photo filtering, and several stability and image-quality improvements.

Today, Adobe is expanding Project Indigo’s toolset once again, with an AI Playground. Here’s Adobe:

Since Indigo is an experimental app, and this is an experiment inside Indigo, we’re offering the AI Playground for free, with no sign-on required. However, this level of free access will be available only to a few percent of our users, and only for a few weeks. Our goal is to learn how people use GenAI-powered editing. Depending on its success we may extend the experiment, or expand the user pool, or run follow-on experiments. If the feature proves popular, we will eventually offer a paid version.

If you’re among the users who get access to Project Indigo’s AI Playground, you’ll see a splash screen inviting you to join the experiment. Adobe says this is optional and, for users who opt in, the company will only collect button presses while using the Playground.

This means Adobe “won’t look at your prompts, or images, or upload them to [its] servers, or use them to train AI models.” The company adds that “since there’s no sign-on, the analytics we gather are anonymous.”

Once the user gets access to Project Indigo’s AI Playground, they will see four new sections. Here is Adobe on each feature, accompanied by examples

Object Editing: “[…] the Object Editing section of our AI Playground contains buttons for distractor removal and defocusing the background to create a shallow depth of field. Within distractor removal is a list of toggles that allow you to remove one or more categories of distractors. You can also add categories by typing (or dictating) what you want removed, and you can save or delete these additions.”

Styles: “Most state-of-the-art GenAI models are capable of mimicking traditional artistic techniques. The Styles menu of our AI Playground contains buttons that will re-render your Indigo photo using a variety of techniques. The buttons we provide represent techniques we have found to be most successful when applied to everyday snapshots.”

Photo Guidance: “Many software products use AI to summarize or critique human-created documents, like email and résumés. We are exploring how to do something similar for photography – to help users learn or advance their photography. Since Indigo is a camera app, our AI analysis can provide both reshooting suggestions as well as editing suggestions.”

Custom Edit: “While our AI Playground provides buttons for many common generative editing operations, our Custom Edit button unlocks the full power of arbitrary text prompts. […] Our Styles buttons let you make photographs look like hand-drawn illustrations. Could we go the other way? The conversion of a drawing, painting, or sculpture into a photograph is not only fun; it’s also instructive. What could the artists have been looking at, which led to the piece they produced? […]”

Adobe says it is currently using Google’s Nano Banana model, which only outputs Standard Dynamic Range (SRD) images, even when the user snaps a shot in High Dynamic Range (HDR). For this reason, the company “built a custom ML model to post-process the standard dynamic range (SDR) images returned by Nano Banana, boosting them back to HDR.” Adobe adds that this is not perfect and relies heavily on the output Nano Banana produces.

One final note: the company explains that Project Indigo’s AI Playground features will only work on images captured with the app. This means that for images imported from elsewhere, the sparkle button that opens the AI Playground will be grayed out.

To learn more about Project Indigo’s AI Playground, follow this link.

content_text: Adobe is rolling out a limited test today that gives select Project Indigo users access to several generative AI-powered photo-editing tools. Here are the details. Adobe’s Project Indigo gets an experimental AI Playground A little more than a year ago, Adobe announced Project Indigo, a free experimental iPhone camera app offering full manual controls and a more natural, SLR-like approach to computational photography. Last April, the company expanded Project Indigo to select iPads and the iPhone 17e, in an update that also introduced a new grid view, photo filtering, and several stability and image-quality improvements. Today, Adobe is expanding Project Indigo’s toolset once again, with an AI Playground. Here’s Adobe: Since Indigo is an experimental app, and this is an experiment inside Indigo, we’re offering the AI Playground for free, with no sign-on required. However, this level of free access will be available only to a few percent of our users, and only for a few weeks. Our goal is to learn how people use GenAI-powered editing. Depending on its success we may extend the experiment, or expand the user pool, or run follow-on experiments. If the feature proves popular, we will eventually offer a paid version. If you’re among the users who get access to Project Indigo’s AI Playground, you’ll see a splash screen inviting you to join the experiment. Adobe says this is optional and, for users who opt in, the company will only collect button presses while using the Playground. This means Adobe “won’t look at your prompts, or images, or upload them to [its] servers, or use them to train AI models.” The company adds that “since there’s no sign-on, the analytics we gather are anonymous.” Once the user gets access to Project Indigo’s AI Playground, they will see four new sections. Here is Adobe on each feature, accompanied by examples Object Editing: “[…] the Object Editing section of our AI Playground contains buttons for distractor removal and defocusing the background to create a shallow depth of field. Within distractor removal is a list of toggles that allow you to remove one or more categories of distractors. You can also add categories by typing (or dictating) what you want removed, and you can save or delete these additions.” Styles: “Most state-of-the-art GenAI models are capable of mimicking traditional artistic techniques. The Styles menu of our AI Playground contains buttons that will re-render your Indigo photo using a variety of techniques. The buttons we provide represent techniques we have found to be most successful when applied to everyday snapshots.” Photo Guidance: “Many software products use AI to summarize or critique human-created documents, like email and résumés. We are exploring how to do something similar for photography – to help users learn or advance their photography. Since Indigo is a camera app, our AI analysis can provide both reshooting suggestions as well as editing suggestions.” Custom Edit: “While our AI Playground provides buttons for many common generative editing operations, our Custom Edit button unlocks the full power of arbitrary text prompts. […] Our Styles buttons let you make photographs look like hand-drawn illustrations. Could we go the other way? The conversion of a drawing, painting, or sculpture into a photograph is not only fun; it’s also instructive. What could the artists have been looking at, which led to the piece they produced? […]” Adobe says it is currently using Google’s Nano Banana model, which only outputs Standard Dynamic Range (SRD) images, even when the user snaps a shot in High Dynamic Range (HDR). For this reason, the company “built a custom ML model to post-process the standard dynamic range (SDR) images returned by Nano Banana, boosting them back to HDR.” Adobe adds that this is not perfect and relies heavily on the output Nano Banana produces. One final note: the company explains that Project Indigo’s AI Playground features will only work on images captured with the app. This means that for images imported from elsewhere, the sparkle button that opens the AI Playground will be grayed out. To learn more about Project Indigo’s AI Playground, follow this link. blocks.html_en: One final note: the company explains that Project Indigo’s AI Playground features will only work on images captured with the app. This means that for images imported from elsewhere, the sparkle button that opens the AI Playground will be grayed out.

sentences.en: This means that for images imported from elsewhere, the sparkle button that opens the AI Playground will be grayed out.
sentences.zh: 这意味着，对于从其他地方导入的图像，打开 AI Playground 的闪光按钮将呈灰色。

content_html_zh:
Adobe 今天推出了一项有限测试，让选定的 Project Indigo 用户可以访问多种由 AI 驱动的生成照片编辑工具。 以下是详细信息。

Adobe 的 Project Indigo 获得实验性 AI Playground

一年多前，Adobe 发布了 Project Indigo，这是一款免费的实验性 iPhone 相机应用程序，提供完整的手动控制和更自然、类似单反相机的计算摄影方法。

去年 4 月，该公司将 Project Indigo 扩展到部分 iPad 和 iPhone 17e，更新中还引入了新的网格视图、照片过滤以及多项稳定性和图像质量改进。

今天，Adobe 再次通过 AI Playground 扩展 Project Indigo 的工具集。 这是 Adob​​e：

由于 Indigo 是一款实验性应用程序，而且这是 Indigo 内部的一项实验，因此我们免费提供 AI Playground，无需登录。 然而，这种级别的免费访问仅适用于我们的一小部分用户，并且只能持续几周。 我们的目标是了解人们如何使用 GenAI 支持的编辑。 根据其成功，我们可能会扩展实验，或扩大用户池，或运行后续实验。 如果该功能很受欢迎，我们最终将提供付费版本。

如果您是有权访问 Project Indigo 的 AI Playground 的用户之一，您将看到一个闪屏，邀请您加入实验。 Adobe 表示这是可选的，对于选择加入的用户，该公司只会收集使用 Playground 时的按钮按下情况。

这意味着 Adob​​e“不会查看您的提示或图像，也不会将它们上传到[其]服务器，或使用它们来训练人工智能模型。” 该公司补充说，“由于没有登录，我们收集的分析是匿名的。”

一旦用户访问 Project Indigo 的 AI Playground，他们将看到四个新部分。 以下是 Adob​​e 对每个功能的介绍，并附有示例

对象编辑：“[...] AI Playground 的对象编辑部分包含用于移除干扰物和散焦背景以创建浅景深的按钮。 干扰项删除中包含一个切换列表，可让您删除一个或多个类别的干扰项。 您还可以通过键入（或口述）要删除的内容来添加类别，并且可以保存或删除这些添加内容。”
风格：“大多数最先进的 GenAI 模型都能够模仿传统艺术技巧。 我们的 AI Playground 的“样式”菜单包含可使用各种技术重新渲染您的 Indigo 照片的按钮。 我们提供的按钮代表了我们发现应用于日常快照时最成功的技术。”
图片指导：“许多软件产品使用人工智能来总结或批评人类创建的文档，例如电子邮件和简历。 我们正在探索如何为摄影做类似的事情——帮助用户学习或提高他们的摄影水平。 由于 Indigo 是一款相机应用程序，因此我们的 AI 分析可以提供重新拍摄建议和编辑建议。”
自定义编辑：“虽然我们的 AI Playground 提供了许多常见生成编辑操作的按钮，但我们的自定义编辑按钮解锁了任意文本提示的全部功能。[...] 我们的样式按钮可让您使照片看起来像手绘插图。 我们可以走另一条路吗？ 将素描、绘画或雕塑转换为照片不仅有趣，而且有趣。这也很有启发性。 艺术家们可能一直在关注什么，从而创作出了他们的作品？ […]”

Adobe 表示，目前正在使用谷歌的 Nano Banana 模型，该模型仅输出标准动态范围 (SRD) 图像，即使用户以高动态范围 (HDR) 拍摄照片也是如此。 因此，该公司“构建了一个自定义 ML 模型来对 Nano Banana 返回的标准动态范围 (SDR) 图像进行后处理，将其恢复为 HDR。” Adobe 补充说，这并不完美，并且在很大程度上依赖于 Nano Banana 产生的输出。

最后一点：该公司解释说，Project Indigo 的 AI Playground 功能仅适用于使用该应用程序捕获的图像。 这意味着，对于从其他地方导入的图像，打开 AI Playground 的闪光按钮将呈灰色。

要了解有关 Project Indigo 的 AI Playground 的更多信息，请点击此链接。
